text:
Perinatal Outreach Program | Kern County, CA
Helping pregnant women in Kern County get the care they need.
Need Help Now?
Call us at: (661) 321-3000
Fax: (661) 868-1291
Who Can Get Help?
The nurse and public health aides provide home visits, health education and outreach activities by doing the followi
You can use our program if:
You’re pregnant (up to 36 weeks)
Reside in Kern County
You can be any age or background
How We Help
Prior to baby's arrival, they are also able to assist with
Our nurses and health aides come to your home and help with:
Before Baby is Born:
Help you find a doctor
Help you find referrals for Medi-Cal and WIC
Help you find rides to your doctor, WIC, or prenatal appointments
Provide prenatal education, including nutrition, mental health, and preparation for labor and delivery
Help you plan for your baby’s care after birth
After Baby is Born:
A nurse checks on you and your baby’s health
Help with doctor visits and shots (immunizations)
Support your recovery after birth and answer questions
Share tips on baby growth and development
Teach about breastfeeding, formula feeding, and newborn care
Connect you to additional community resources and supportive services as needed
Families receive monthly in-home visits, with additional support by phone, from pregnancy through the baby's first birthday.
How to Join
Referrals are open to everyone.  Individuals can self-refer, and healthcare providers or community agencies may
refer a client to POP
by calling us at (661) 321-3000 or faxing us at (661) 868-1291.
